metadata.description: MIT LLM/AI Agents UROP Lab Database

andreas_lingo.lab_focus: LLM reasoning, in-context learning, language-guided planning, multimodal interpretability

pi.name: Jacob Andreas
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS

andreas_lingo.research_summary: Leads MIT's most prolific LLM lab. Work spans LLM reasoning and evaluation, self-steering language models, in-context learning, multimodal interpretability agents, and language-guided robot planning. Extensive collaborations with Tenenbaum, Kim, Levy, Torralba.
key_papers: DisCIPL: Self-Steering Language Models (compositional inference with LLMs)
Breakpoint: Evaluating LLM agents via environment perturbations
MAIA: Multimodal Automated Interpretability Agent (ICML 2024)
Reasoning or Reciting? Capabilities of LLMs through counterfactual evals (NAACL 2024)
The Consensus Game: LM generation via equilibrium search (ICLR 2024 Spotlight)
OpenEstimate: Evaluating LLMs on reasoning under uncertainty (ICLR 2026)
Log-Augmented Generation: scaling test-time reasoning (ICLR 2026)
Learning Linear Attention in Polynomial Time (NeurIPS 2025 Oral)
A spectral understanding of LoRA fine-tuning (NeurIPS 2025)
EWoK: Evaluating basic world knowledge in LLMs
Eliciting Human Preferences with Language Models (ICLR 2025)
Test-time training for few-shot learning (ICML 2025)
Beyond binary rewards: training LMs to reason about uncertainty
Generative AI in the Era of Alternative Facts (EMNLP 2024)
LaMPP: Language Models as Probabilistic Priors for Perception and Action
Visual grounding helps learn word meanings (NAACL 2024 Best Paper)
Whale communication analysis with LLMs (Nature Communications 2024)
andreas_lingo.urop_relevance: High - large lab with active undergraduate involvement; co-leads SQI Language and Thought Mission which offers LLM UROPs

kim_yoon.lab_focus: LLM architectures, efficient transformers, self-adapting LLMs, language model analysis

pi.name: Yoon Kim
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS (tenured effective July 2025)

kim_yoon.research_summary: Core LLM architecture researcher. Works on efficient LLM architectures (gated linear attention), the semantic hub hypothesis (how LLMs share representations across languages), self-adapting LLMs (SEAL), Co-LLM collaborative decoding. Co-teaches NLP with Andreas.
key_papers: SEAL: Self-Adapting LLMs via RL-based self-editing (NeurIPS, with UROP co-author)
Semantic Hub Hypothesis: Language models share semantic representations across languages and modalities
Co-LLM: collaborative decoding combining general and specialized LLMs
Gated Linear Attention Transformers: efficient alternative to softmax attention
Reasoning or Reciting? (NAACL 2024, with Andreas)
In-context language learning: Architectures and algorithms (with Andreas)
Beyond binary rewards: training LMs to reason about uncertainty
kim_yoon.urop_relevance: High - SEAL paper had a UROP co-author funded by Stevens Fund

khattab_nlp@mit.lab_focus: LLM programming, retrieval-augmented generation, declarative AI systems, language model compilers

pi.name: Omar Khattab
pi.title: Assistant Professor, EECS (joined Fall 2025)

khattab_nlp@mit.research_summary: Creator of DSPy (first LLM program compiler, 500K+ monthly downloads) and ColBERT retrieval model. Pioneer of retrieval-augmented generation. Research on composing retrieval and reasoning, automated RAG evaluation (ARES), and LLM programming abstractions. One of MIT's newest and most directly LLM-focused hires - building lab now.
key_papers: DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines (ICLR 2024 Spotlight)
DSPy Assertions: Computational Constraints for Self-Refining LM Pipelines
STORM: Writing Wikipedia-like Articles from Scratch with LLMs (NAACL 2024)
ARES: Automated Evaluation Framework for RAG Systems (NAACL 2024)
PAPILLON: Privacy Preservation from LLM Ensembles (NAACL 2025)
GEPA: Reflective Prompt Evolution Can Outperform RL (2025)
ColBERT-serve: Efficient Multi-Stage Memory-Mapped Scoring (ECIR 2025)
WARP: Scalable Retrieval Infrastructure (SIGIR 2025 Best Paper)
FreshStack: Building Realistic Benchmarks for Retrieval on Technical Documents
Grounding by Trying: LLMs with RL-Enhanced Retrieval (ICLR 2025)
PATH and IReRa: state-of-the-art LM pipeline systems
MIPRO: Multi-prompt Instruction Proposal Optimizer (EMNLP 2024)
BetterTogether: improving LM pipelines (EMNLP 2024)
khattab_nlp@mit.urop_relevance: VERY HIGH - brand new lab, actively recruiting, most likely to need UROPs

han_mit.lab_focus: Efficient LLM inference, quantization, long-context LLMs, on-device AI, vision-language models

pi.name: Song Han
pi.title: Associate Professor (with tenure), EECS

han_mit.research_summary: Leading researcher on efficient LLM inference and deployment. AWQ (19M+ HuggingFace downloads, MLSys 2024 Best Paper), SmoothQuant (W8A8 quantization), StreamingLLM (infinite-length generation), DuoAttention, QServe, LServe, VILA vision-language models. Methods integrated into NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM.
key_papers: AWQ: Activation-aware Weight Quantization for LLM Compression (MLSys 2024 Best Paper)
SmoothQuant: Accurate and Efficient Post-Training Quantization for LLMs (ICML 2023)
StreamingLLM: Efficient Streaming LM with Attention Sinks
StreamingVLM: streaming-aware KV cache for infinite video understanding
QServe: W4A8KV4 Quantization for large-scale LLM Serving
LServe: Efficient Long-sequence LLM Serving
DuoAttention: reducing LLM decoding/pre-filling memory with retrieval/streaming heads
Quest: query-aware sparsity in long-context KV cache
COAT: Memory efficient FP8 training
VILA: Vision-Language Models on the edge with AWQ
DistriFusion: multi-GPU diffusion model acceleration (CVPR 2024 Highlight)
VLaSH: Real-Time VLAs via Future-state-aware Asynchronous Inference
Four Over Six: NVFP4 Quantization with Adaptive Block Scaling
Radial Attention: O(nlogn) Sparse Attention for Long Video Generation (NeurIPS 2025)
han_mit.urop_relevance: High - large active lab with many students

solar-lezama_computer-aided.lab_focus: Program synthesis, LLMs for code generation, neurosymbolic programming, AI agents for software engineering

pi.name: Armando Solar-Lezama
pi.title: Distinguished Professor of Computing; CSAIL Associate Director & COO

solar-lezama_computer-aided.research_summary: Intersection of program synthesis and AI. Extensive work on LLMs for code generation, neurosymbolic programming, and AI agents for software engineering. Developed EnCompass (AI agent search framework), combines LLMs with first-order logic provers (LINC).
key_papers: EnCompass: AI agent search framework for software engineering
LINC: Combining LLMs with First-Order Logic Provers (EMNLP 2023, with Levy)
Limits of LLMs for reasoning tasks
Neurosymbolic programming with language models
LLM-guided program repair and synthesis

kraska_data.lab_focus: Agentic AI for data systems, LLM-powered data management, generative AI for databases

pi.name: Tim Kraska
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS; College of Computing Distinguished Professor

kraska_data.research_summary: Co-directs MIT Generative AI Impact Consortium. Develops declarative frameworks for AI workloads (Palimpzest), created KramaBench (first benchmark for data-science agents), explores agentic systems with long-context optimization and graph-based reasoning.
key_papers: Palimpzest: Declarative framework for AI workloads
KramaBench: First benchmark for data-science agents
Long-context LLM optimization for agentic systems
Graph-based reasoning with language models
kraska_data.urop_relevance: High - leads MGAIC which supports undergraduate research

agrawal_improbable.lab_focus: Foundation models for robotics, self-adapting LLMs, robot manipulation

pi.name: Pulkit Agrawal
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS

agrawal_improbable.research_summary: Co-developed SEAL (Self-Adapting LLMs) with Yoon Kim, enabling LLMs to permanently learn from new inputs via RL-based self-editing. Also works on world models for robotics from human videos and foundation models for robotic manipulation.
key_papers: SEAL: Self-Adapting LLMs via RL-based self-editing (NeurIPS, UROP co-author)
World Models for Robotics from Human Videos
Foundation models for robotic manipulation
agrawal_improbable.urop_relevance: VERY HIGH - SEAL paper included UROP student; has active UROP listed on ELX

elx_urops.title: World Models for Robotics from Human Videos
elx_urops.overview:
The main bottleneck in the path to general purpose robotics is real world embodied data. Two frontier research directions are world modelling and human pose estimation. Together, these two tasks solved properly can turn Youtube videos into a simulator, useful for training robots through reinforcement learning. 

In this project, you will train a simulator for manipulation from human videos. You will investigate state of the art practices for compute-efficient video training (eg see Test time tra...

torralba_torralba.lab_focus: Multimodal AI, vision-language models, automated interpretability, multi-agent debate for LLM factuality

pi.name: Antonio Torralba
pi.title: Delta Electronics Professor; Faculty Head of AI+D

torralba_torralba.research_summary: Multimodal AI pioneer. Published MAIA (Multimodal Automated Interpretability Agent), improving LLM factuality through multi-agent debate, vision check-ups for language models, and composable diffusion models. Active at intersection of vision-language foundation models.
key_papers: MAIA: Multimodal Automated Interpretability Agent (ICML 2024)
Multi-agent debate for improving LLM factuality
Vision check-ups for language models
Composable diffusion models
A spectral understanding of LoRA fine-tuning (NeurIPS 2025, with Andreas)
LucidSim: synthetic robot training data (with Isola)

isola_isola.lab_focus: Generative AI, diffusion models, representation convergence across modalities, synthetic data for robotics

pi.name: Phillip Isola
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS

isola_isola.research_summary: Generative AI and the Platonic Representation Hypothesis (how multimodal models converge on shared representations). Uses generative AI to create synthetic robot training data (LucidSim). Research connects foundation models across vision and language modalities.
key_papers: The Platonic Representation Hypothesis (multimodal convergence)
LucidSim: synthetic robot training data with generative AI
Diffusion model advances for generation and perception

he_kaiming.lab_focus: Visual foundation models, self-supervised learning, scalable representation learning

pi.name: Kaiming He
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS (joined February 2024)

he_kaiming.research_summary: Pioneering deep learning researcher (ResNet, Masked Autoencoders). Works on visual foundation models, self-supervised learning approaches parallel to LLM pre-training. Sponsors 'AI + Chaotic System' UROP using foundation models.
key_papers: Foundation models for chaotic systems and PDE solvers
Masked Autoencoders (MAE) extensions
Visual representation learning at scale
he_kaiming.urop_relevance: HIGH - has active ELX UROP listing

elx_urops.title: AI + Chaotic System
elx_urops.overview:
This project explores AI methods for chaotic systems, which are inherently unstable: even a tiny perturbation in initial conditions can lead to dramatically different outcomes, fundamentally limiting predictability. Our goal is to develop foundational machine learning models that serve as universal representations for ergodic systems, with applications in weather forecasting, climate engineering, and extreme event prediction.

Large-scale simulations in fields such as turbulence and lithography ...

liang_multisensory.lab_focus: Multimodal AI, RAG-driven multimodal LLMs, social bias in language models, AI for healthcare

pi.name: Paul Pu Liang
pi.title: Assistant Professor, Media Arts & Sciences and EECS (joined September 2024)

liang_multisensory.research_summary: Built theoretical foundations and large-scale resources for multimodal AI widely used in generative models. Published definitive survey on multimodal ML (Computing Surveys 2024). RAG-driven multimodal LLMs, social bias in language models. Forbes 30 Under 30. Teaches 'How to AI (Almost) Anything.'
key_papers: Foundations and Recent Trends in Multimodal Machine Learning (Computing Surveys 2024, definitive survey)
RAG-driven multimodal LLMs for ECG interpretation
Social bias in language models
Multimodal AI applications in healthcare and robotics
How to AI (Almost) Anything (course materials)
liang_multisensory.urop_relevance: VERY HIGH - brand new lab, actively recruiting

rives_rives.lab_focus: Protein language models, biological sequence generation, evolutionary-scale modeling

pi.name: Alex Rives
pi.title: Assistant Professor, EECS (joined September 2024)

rives_rives.research_summary: Pioneer of protein language models. Founded ESM team at Meta. ESM3 (Science 2025) is a frontier generative language model that simulated 500M years of evolution and generated a novel fluorescent protein. Demonstrates how transformer architectures extend to biological sequences.
key_papers: ESM3: Simulating 500 million years of evolution with a language model (Science 2025)
ESM-2: Protein language model scaling laws
Biological sequence generation and protein design with LLMs
rives_rives.urop_relevance: HIGH - brand new lab, actively recruiting

barzilay_nlp.lab_focus: NLP for drug discovery, clinical AI, computational chemistry

pi.name: Regina Barzilay
pi.title: School of Engineering Distinguished Professor of AI & Health

barzilay_nlp.research_summary: Co-leads MIT CSAIL NLP Group. Pioneer in NLP applied to drug discovery, clinical AI, and computational chemistry. Foundational NLP work includes decipherment of dead languages, cross-lingual transfer.
key_papers: NLP for drug discovery and molecular generation
Clinical AI with language models
Cross-lingual and cross-domain NLP transfer

pi.name: Regina Barzilay
pi.title: School of Engineering Distinguished Professor of AI & Health

barzilay_mit.research_summary: Co-leads MIT CSAIL NLP Group. Pioneer in NLP applied to drug discovery, clinical AI, and computational chemistry. Foundational NLP work includes decipherment of dead languages, cross-lingual transfer.
key_papers: NLP for drug discovery and molecular generation
Clinical AI with language models
Cross-lingual and cross-domain NLP transfer

jaakkola_nlp.lab_focus: Statistical inference, generative models, NLP for computational chemistry

pi.name: Tommi Jaakkola
pi.title: Thomas Siebel Professor, EECS and IDSS

jaakkola_nlp.research_summary: Co-leads MIT CSAIL NLP Group. Work spans statistical inference, generative models (PFGM++), conformal language modeling, and cooperative rationalization. His methods underlie modern approaches to scalable inference relevant to LLM training.
key_papers: PFGM++: generative model advances
Conformal language modeling
Learning Diffusion Models with Flexible Representation Guidance (NeurIPS 2025)
NLP for computational chemistry and functional genomics

sontag_clinical.lab_focus: Clinical NLP with LLMs, zero-shot clinical extraction, collaborative LLMs

pi.name: David Sontag
pi.title: Professor, EECS

sontag_clinical.research_summary: Clinical NLP with LLMs. Developed zero-shot clinical information extraction using GPT-style models. Co-authored Co-LLM (combining general and expert LLMs for improved factuality).
key_papers: Co-LLM: combining general and expert LLMs
Zero-shot clinical information extraction using LLMs
LLMs for deciphering clinical notes and extracting medication regimens

kaelbling_learning.lab_focus: LLMs for hierarchical robot planning, embodied AI, foundation models for decision-making

pi.name: Leslie Kaelbling
pi.title: Panasonic Professor, EECS

kaelbling_learning.research_summary: Uses LLMs for hierarchical robot planning. Developed HiP framework where an LLM serves as the language reasoner in a three-pronged planning system. Integrates foundation models into embodied decision-making.
key_papers: HiP: Hierarchical Planning with LLM language reasoner for robot manipulation
Foundation models for embodied decision-making
Language-guided robot planning and task decomposition

rus_distributed.lab_focus: LLMs for robot task generation, generative AI for robotics, whale communication analysis

pi.name: Daniela Rus
pi.title: Andrew & Erna Viterbi Professor; CSAIL Director

rus_distributed.research_summary: CSAIL Director integrating generative AI into robotics. Lab uses LLMs for robot task generation (GenSim), applies language models to sperm whale communication analysis. Co-founded LiquidAI.
key_papers: GenSim: LLMs for robot task generation
Whale communication analysis with language models (Nature Communications 2024)
LGA: Language-Guided Abstraction for robot planning (with Shah)

shah_interactive.lab_focus: Language-guided robot planning, human-robot collaboration, LLM-driven task abstraction

pi.name: Julie Shah
pi.title: Professor; Head, AeroAstro Department

shah_interactive.research_summary: Co-authored LGA (Language-Guided Abstraction) framework that uses natural language to help LLMs create planning representations for robotics. Bridges language models and embodied AI.
key_papers: LGA: Language-Guided Abstraction for robot planning (CoRL 2024)
ALGAE: Adaptive Language-Guided Abstraction from Contrastive Explanations (with Bobu)
Preference-Conditioned Language-Guided Abstraction (HRI 2024)

bobu_clear.lab_focus: LLMs for robotics and human-robot interaction, VLM-guided robot learning

pi.name: Andreea Bobu
pi.title: Assistant Professor, AeroAstro (joined July 2024)

bobu_clear.research_summary: Uses LLMs extensively for robotics and human-robot interaction. Developed ALGAE (Adaptive Language-Guided Abstraction from Contrastive Explanations, CoRL 2024). Uses LLMs to infer task goals from open-ended dialog. MGAIC-funded project on VLM-guided robot learning augmentation.
key_papers: ALGAE: Adaptive Language-Guided Abstraction from Contrastive Explanations (CoRL 2024)
LLMs for inferring task goals from open-ended dialog
VLM-guided robot learning augmentation (MGAIC)
Designing Jet Engines with AI: Analyzing Human-LLM Collaboration
bobu_clear.urop_relevance: VERY HIGH - new lab, has active ELX UROP listing, MGAIC-funded

elx_urops.title: Designing Jet Engines with AI: Analyzing Human-LLM Collaboration
elx_urops.overview:
Generative AI is changing how we approach engineering, but how do people actually interact with these systems over sustained periods? This UROP aims to answer that by evaluating students’ usage of Large Language Models (LLMs) during complex engineering tasks.

You will be working with a first-of-its-kind longitudinal dataset collected during IAP, tracking student teams as they interacted with LLMs to design and build gas turbine engines.

As a UROP on this project, your core responsibilities wil...

mansinghka_probabilistic.lab_focus: Probabilistic programming for LLM control, self-steering language models, generative AI for databases

pi.name: Vikash Mansinghka
pi.title: Principal Research Scientist, BCS / CSAIL

mansinghka_probabilistic.research_summary: Developed LLaMPPL, a programming language for controlling language models central to the DisCIPL framework. Co-authored work on self-steering language models and GenSQL (generative AI for databases).
key_papers: LLaMPPL: programming language for controlling language models
DisCIPL: self-steering language models framework
GenSQL: generative AI for databases

cafarella_data.lab_focus: LLM reasoning, data science agents, log-augmented generation

pi.name: Michael Cafarella
pi.title: Principal Research Scientist, CSAIL

cafarella_data.research_summary: Co-authored Log-Augmented Generation (scaling test-time reasoning in LLMs, ICLR 2026) and OpenEstimate (evaluating LLMs on reasoning under uncertainty, ICLR 2026). Works with Andreas on LLM reasoning systems.
key_papers: Log-Augmented Generation: scaling test-time reasoning in LLMs (ICLR 2026)
OpenEstimate: evaluating LLMs on reasoning under uncertainty (ICLR 2026)
Palimpzest: declarative AI workloads (with Kraska)

oliva_oliva.lab_name: Oliva Lab / MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab
oliva_oliva.lab_focus: LLM fine-tuning, vision-language models, parameter-efficient methods

pi.name: Aude Oliva
pi.title: Senior Research Scientist; MIT Director, MIT-IBM Watson AI Lab

oliva_oliva.research_summary: Published Trans-LoRA (NeurIPS 2024), a data-free transferable parameter-efficient finetuning method for LLMs. Supervised UROP students on data-free LLM fine-tuning. Also works on vision-language models (ConMe, NeurIPS 2024).
key_papers: Trans-LoRA: data-free transferable parameter-efficient LLM finetuning (NeurIPS 2024)
ConMe: Vision-Language Models (NeurIPS 2024)

rinard_rinard.lab_focus: LLM world models, program repair with LLMs, simulations of reality in LLMs

pi.name: Martin Rinard
pi.title: Professor, EECS

rinard_rinard.research_summary: Discovered simulations of reality developing deep within LLMs, indicating understanding beyond simple mimicry. Works on LLM world models and program repair. Co-developed GenSQL with Mansinghka.
key_papers: LLMs develop their own understanding of reality as language abilities improve
GenSQL: generative AI for databases

karger_haystack.lab_focus: LLMex project, AI-based memory prosthetics, information management with LLMs

pi.name: David Karger
pi.title: Professor, EECS

karger_haystack.research_summary: Co-leads the LLMex project implementing Vannevar Bush's Memex vision using LLMs - building AI-based memory prosthetics that use LLMs to help individuals track information and proactively suggest relevant content.

AI-based memory prosthetics with language models

pi.name: Joshua Tenenbaum
pi.title: Professor of Computational Cognitive Science

tenenbaum_computational.research_summary: Bridges cognitive science and AI. Co-leads major work on neurosymbolic approaches (LILO, Ada, DisCIPL) combining LLMs with symbolic planning. Studies LLMs for building rational agents and common-sense reasoning. MGAIC-funded 'Rational Generative AI' project.
key_papers: DisCIPL: self-steering language models (with Andreas, Mansinghka)
LILO: Language-inspired library of operators
Rational agents that explore and act like people (ICLR 2026)
Language and experience: computational model of social learning (ICLR 2026)
Loose LIPS: Battleship with language-informed program sampling (CogSci 2024)
MGAIC Rational Generative AI project

levy_computational.lab_focus: Computational linguistics, LLM evaluation, LLM world knowledge, language processing

pi.name: Roger Levy
pi.title: Professor, BCS; Chair of MIT Faculty (2025-2027)

levy_computational.research_summary: Central to computational linguistics and NLP. Published LINC (combining LLMs with first-order logic provers, EMNLP 2023), studies prompting vs. probability measurements in LLMs, and evaluates LLM world knowledge (EWoK). Extensively collaborates with Andreas, Kim, Solar-Lezama.
key_papers: LINC: Combining LLMs with first-order logic provers (EMNLP 2023)
EWoK: Evaluating basic world knowledge in language models
Prompting vs. probability measurements in LLMs
Learning phonotactics from linguistic informants (ICML 2024 Oral, with Andreas)

maes_fluid.lab_focus: LLMs for mixed reality, wearable AI memory, LLM agents, cognitive enhancement

maes_fluid.research_summary: One of the most active Media Lab faculty in LLM research. LLMR (LLM for Mixed Reality, CHI 2024), Memoro (wearable AI memory using LLMs), studies of LLM agent behavior vs humans (NeurIPS 2024), RAG-driven multimodal LLMs for ECG, LLM-based language learning chatbots.
key_papers: LLMR: LLM for Real-time Prompting of Interactive Worlds (CHI 2024 Honorable Mention)
Memoro: Wearable AI memory assistant using LLMs
How LLM agents deviate from human choices (NeurIPS 2024)

LLM-based language learning chatbots for growth mindset

raskar_camera.research_summary: PI of NANDA, building foundational infrastructure for an 'Internet of AI Agents' extending Anthropic's MCP and Google's Agent-to-Agent protocols. Also leads LLM agent benchmarking and multimodal data selection.

elx_urops.title: LLM Agent Benchmarking & Multi-Modal Data Selection
elx_urops.overview: As Large Language Models (LLMs) evolve into autonomous AI agents capable of complex task execution, static benchmarks/leaderboards fail to capture how these AI systems will perform in real world scenarios. Our research group at the MIT Media Lab is working on building  infrastructure to rigorously evaluate agents via adaptive interviews and improve them via high-value data selection. We are seeking UROPs to co-design and implement components of these automated evaluators in collaboration with Ph...
elx_urops.prerequisites: We are looking for students with strong coding skills (Python, Bash, Git, PyTorch, LLM APIs) and familiarity with machine learning (e.g. 6.790, 6.7960 or equivalent experience).

paradiso_responsive.lab_focus: Generative AI for music, LLMs for mixed reality, AI agents for collaborative robots

pi.name: Joseph Paradiso
pi.title: Alexander W. Dreyfoos Professor; Academic Head, MAS

paradiso_responsive.research_summary: Co-authored publications on LLMs for mixed reality (LLMR) and enabling waypoint generation for collaborative robots using LLMs. Develops real-time generative AI agents for musical improvisation.
key_papers: LLMR: LLMs for Mixed Reality (with Maes)
Waypoint generation for collaborative robots using LLMs
Real-time generative AI agents for musical improvisation (MGAIC, with Anna Huang)

elx_urops.title: Real-Time Generative AI for Musical Improvisation - UROP
elx_urops.overview:
The Responsive Environments group at the MIT Media Lab is working with multiple professional artists (most recently, GRAMMY-winning visiting artist Jordan Rudess) to develop real-time generative AI agents that can engage in live musical improvisation. We are looking for highly motivated and experienced UROPs, with extensive experience in Machine Learning and Generative AI to help us develop the project. Potential projects include:

- Developing optimized local AI pipelines for real-time processi...

huang_huang.lab_focus: Music Transformer, real-time collaborative AI agents for music, generative AI

pi.name: Anna Huang
pi.title: Associate Professor, EECS & Music and Theater Arts (joined September 2024)

huang_huang.research_summary: Created Music Transformer (ICLR 2018), first successful adaptation of transformer architecture to music. Developing real-time collaborative AI agents for live musical improvisation (jam_bots, MGAIC-funded). 8 years at Google Brain/DeepMind Magenta team.
key_papers: Music Transformer: Generating Music with Long-Term Structure (ICLR 2018)
jam_bots: Real-time collaborative AI agents for musical improvisation (MGAIC)
Developing symbiotic virtuosity with AI-augmented instruments
huang_huang.urop_relevance: HIGH - new lab, MGAIC-funded, actively recruiting

katz_infolab.lab_focus: Natural language understanding, agentic AI, programming by example

pi.name: Boris Katz
pi.title: Principal Research Scientist, CSAIL

katz_infolab.research_summary: Created START natural language system. Current research includes vision-language models, agentic AI through programming by example. Co-PI on 'Programming by Example for Agentic AI' UROP.

Vision-language models for question answering
Neural network guidance for NLU
katz_infolab.urop_relevance: HIGH - has active ELX UROP on Agentic AI

elx_urops.title: Programming by Example for Agentic AI
elx_urops.overview:
How can we make the computational power of programming more accessible to nontechnical people?

Programming by Example (PBE) is a technique for instructing computers how to perform procedures, by having a person demonstrate the steps on concrete examples. The system records the steps, and can replay them on a different example. This is like a so-called "macro recorder", but the user can also instruct the system to generalize the procedure, so it can work in cases that are different from, but ana...

kellis_mit.lab_focus: Genome language models, AI agents for biological data exploration, computational genomics

pi.name: Manolis Kellis
pi.title: Professor of Computer Science, EECS

kellis_mit.category: Language Models for Biology / AI Agents
kellis_mit.research_summary: Teaches ML for Computational Biology covering genome language models and foundation models for drug discovery. Multiple AI agent UROPs (Mantis platform) on ELX. Uses transformer architectures on genomic sequences.
key_papers: AI agents for biological data exploration (Mantis platform)
Genome language models for drug discovery
Machine Learning for Computational Biology (course covering LLMs for biology)

elx_urops.title: Building Interactive AI Agents for Biological Data Exploration in Mantis
elx_urops.overview: Join our team in creating advanced AI agents that enhance the Mantis platform—a cutting-edge tool designed to revolutionize how researchers interact with complex biological data. Mantis integrates structured and unstructured datasets into dynamic, interactive visualizations, offering a versatile environment for data-driven discovery. In this project, you will help develop AI agents that provide real-time data orchestration, computational augmentation, and interactive, context-aware insights to g...

elx_urops.title: Building an Interactive AI Agents Compute Augmentation platform for Biological Data Exploration and Quantitative Integration
elx_urops.overview: This project is dedicated to creating a versatile data science workbench that integrates advanced visualization, AI-driven analysis, and interactive computation within a unified platform. A central feature of this aim is the Sandbox Environment, a Jupyter-like computational tool that allows users to dynamically explore biological data, perform custom analyses, and develop novel insights directly within the Mantis cognitive maps. This project aims to empower researchers by providing a seamless in...

elx_urops.title: Interactive Idea Navigator Collaborative Space: AI-Enhanced Team Interaction and Collaboration
elx_urops.overview: The future of work is rapidly evolving with the advent of deep learning and artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. This one-year undergraduate research project aims to investigate the potential impact of AI-powered productivity and collaboration tools, new modes of human-machine collaboration, and human-machine interaction incorporating uncertainty, trust, and behavioral biases. The research will involve exploring and developing novel approaches to enhance productivity and collaboration in t...

elx_urops.title: Tangible AI
elx_urops.overview: Join us in developing Tangible AI interactions. Sketchnotes are visual thoughts in motion—what if they could inspire the next generation of innovators? We are currently working on an interface that turns old sketchnotes into interactive, idea-sparking tools using a custom-trained AI. We’re looking for a curious, motivated student to help us prototype, test, and expand this human-AI co-ideation platform. If you are interested in shaping interactions across time and are passionate about creativity...

elx_urops.title: Latent Lab: Reimagining the File System with AI
elx_urops.overview:
The Viral Communications group at the MIT Media Lab is looking for students excited to challenge one of the oldest metaphors in computing: the desktop. Our sub-project, Filescape, extends Latent Lab (https://www.media.mit.edu/projects/latent-lab/overview/) into a new kind of file system - one that organizes and visualizes content associatively rather than hierarchically.

We already have a working system, but we’re seeking collaborators to help push it further. The goals are twofold:

Technical ...

amin_resilient.lab_focus: Agentic AI for power grid, LLM multi-agent systems for infrastructure

pi.title: Associate Professor, Civil & Environmental Engineering; LIDS PI

amin_resilient.research_summary: PI of 'Agentic AI for Streamlining the Power Grid Interconnection Process' - using LLMs and multi-agent AI systems to streamline critical parts of grid interconnection. Developed multi-agent workflow with structured LLM agents.
key_papers: Agentic AI for power grid interconnection (multi-agent LLM systems)
A Bayesian Framework for Human-AI Collaboration
amin_resilient.urop_relevance: HIGH - has active ELX UROP on Agentic AI

elx_urops.title: Agentic AI for Streamlining the Power Grid Interconnection Process
elx_urops.overview:
Project Background:
 The U.S. electric grid is facing unprecedented interconnection backlogs, with more than 2,600 GW of generation and storage projects waiting in queues nationwide. Under FERC Order 2023 and related reforms, grid operators (ISOs/RTOs) have transitioned to cluster-based studies, yet bottlenecks remain due to repeated restudies, document-heavy workflows, heterogeneous modeling requirements, and coordination overhead.
This project explores how Large Language Models (LLMs) and mult...
elx_urops.prerequisites:
Required:
•	Strong Python programming skills
•	Experience building structured software systems (not just scripts)
•	Interest in LLM-based systems and agentic workflows
Highly Preferred:
•	Experience with agent frameworks (e.g., AutoGen, LangChain, CrewAI, etc.)
•	Exposure to optimization (linear programming, mixed-integer programming)
•	Experience with UI frameworks (PySide6, Streamlit, etc.)
Beneficial but Not Required:
•	Exposure to power systems, energy systems, or electricity markets

azizan_azizan.lab_focus: Adaptive LLM reasoning, instance-adaptive scaling, process reward models

pi.name: Navid Azizan
pi.title: Assistant Professor, Mechanical Engineering; LIDS/IDSS

azizan_azizan.research_summary: Developed adaptive reasoning methods for LLMs - instance-adaptive scaling that dynamically adjusts computational budget based on question difficulty, reducing computation by 50% while maintaining accuracy. Work on process reward model calibration.
key_papers: Instance-adaptive scaling for LLM reasoning (50% computation reduction)
Process reward model calibration for LLMs

pentland_human.research_summary: Co-authored LLMs for learning legal concepts (ACL 2024). Leads the Data Provenance Initiative - massive audit of 1,800+ AI text datasets tracing provenance for training language models. Research on authenticated delegation and authorized AI agents.
key_papers: Data Provenance Initiative: audit of 1,800+ AI training datasets
Leveraging LLMs for Learning Complex Legal Concepts (ACL 2024)

elx_urops.title: Digital Frontlines
elx_urops.overview: Since the full-scale Russian invasion of Ukraine in 2022, Russian propaganda has pervasively extended into frontline territories through identity-based influence operations (IOs). This research examines how the media content of Russian IOs spreads on Ukrainian Telegram channels and how it impacts people's sense of identity.
